illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A runtime could mint a Request from incomplete or quarantined draft data.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Promote only from ready_to_promote after explicit readiness proof and settled promotion.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#4 Canonical ingest and request promotion

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Booking or pharmacy ambiguity could be copied into Request.workflowState and mask the milestone-only contract.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Keep confirmation ambiguity on ExternalConfirmationGate or case-local state; canonical workflow stays milestone-only.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 54 - Request.workflowState incorrectly mixed workflow milestones with reconciliation states

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A child domain or UI could close the Request while blockers are still open.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Only LifecycleCoordinator may derive closed after a persisted RequestClosureRecord with an empty blocker set.
source_refs: phase-3-the-human-checkpoint.md#3A Triage contract and workspace state model

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: An implementation could treat urgent requirement as if the urgent advice was already issued and let the case drift back to routine safety.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Keep urgent_diversion_required durable until UrgentDiversionSettlement proves issuance; only new evidence may change safety after that.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Auth or support flows could stamp patientRef directly and bypass IdentityBinding governance.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: patientRef remains nullable until IdentityBinding settles; claim requires verified binding plus current session and route tuple.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Identity repair would be flattened into the episode lifecycle and make a temporary privacy freeze look like a lifecycle stage.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Keep identity repair on IdentityRepairCase and Episode blocker refs; Episode.state remains open/resolved/archived.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Telephony flow could promote partially captured evidence into routine safety.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Only a settled readiness assessment may declare safety_usable after the required recording/transcript/structured capture conditions are satisfied.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A telephony operator could bypass the continuity proof boundary and issue a seeded continuation anyway.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Continuation eligibility may narrow or force manual handling, but it may not bypass AccessGrantService or widen continuity scope outside the proven lineage.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A system could revoke patient binding without opening repair and freeze the lineage invisibly.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Wrong-patient correction must pass through correction_pending and repair/branch disposition objects before release or revocation settles.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Routes could act as if a restricted session exists without ever establishing current identity and tuple authority.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: All post-auth posture must settle through SessionEstablishmentDecision before restrictions or writable actionability are exposed.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Authentication success could be mistaken for writable patient or operator authority.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Writable route authority requires current capability decision, session, live RouteIntentBinding, and publishable runtime binding.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A continuation or callback URL could be treated as valid without exact-once redemption settlement.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Any first successful presentation must settle through AccessGrantRedemptionRecord before session creation or route re-entry continues.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: same_episode_candidate could silently collapse or attach work without explicit review or continuity witness.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Only DuplicateResolutionDecision with a valid decision class and witness may settle attach, retry, or related linkage.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Accepted but degraded progress could be silently dropped or marked closed without review.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Fallback review must remain explicit and closure-blocking until recovered, superseded by governed manual action, or explicitly closed under policy.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A stale owner could silently resume mutation after expiry.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Lease expiry must open stale-owner recovery and only a new compare-and-set acquire or supervised takeover may mint fresh ownershipEpoch and fencingToken.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A cached close decision could be reused after blockers, lineage epoch, or confirmation ambiguity drift.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Each close decision must be recomputed against the current requiredLineageEpoch and blocker refs rather than replaying stale calmness.
illegal_transition_rows.notes: The same row captures stale close replay risk rather than a literal state transition.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: UI or booking logic could jump from selection to booked reassurance without a real hold or authoritative confirmation.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Selection and reservation truth stay explicit: soft_selected is never exclusivity or final booking truth.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Transport or provider acceptance alone could be mistaken for confirmed booking or dispatch truth.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Business success requires gate confirmation with required hard matches and sufficient corroboration, not transport acceptance alone.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A route could regain writable posture just because the UI still has a CTA, even though its tuple is stale.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Any tuple drift requires fresh explicit disambiguation and a reissued RouteIntentBinding before writable posture resumes.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#25A Any writable command must prove one exact target tuple

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: UI could collapse local acknowledgement or processing acceptance into final reassurance.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Calm success requires authoritativeOutcomeState = settled plus the required proof class and any command-following projection truth.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A client cache or stale route contract could reopen mutating affordances after tuple drift.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Writable or calm posture may return only after a fresh runtime binding proves exact publication parity and current continuity proof.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Triage could collapse straight to closed after local endpoint choice or local acknowledgement.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Triage closes only after authoritative endpoint settlement and task completion settlement; canonical request closure remains coordinator-owned.
source_refs: phase-3-the-human-checkpoint.md#3G Direct resolution, downstream handoff seeds, and reopen mechanics

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A superseded preview or approval could still launch new downstream work.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Replacement epochs must be explicit; stale epochs remain provenance only and cannot authorize preview, approval, submit, or downstream seeds.
source_refs: phase-3-the-human-checkpoint.md#3E Endpoint decision engine and resolution model

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: An old approval could still authorize changed notes, endpoint, evidence, duplicate resolution, or publication posture.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Material change supersedes the checkpoint and requires fresh approval on the replacement epoch.
source_refs: phase-3-the-human-checkpoint.md#3F Human approval checkpoint and urgent escalation path
illegal_transition_rows.notes: This row captures stale approval reuse rather than a literal state transition.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: An expired or superseded reply could silently mint new evidence and reopen routine queue flow.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: expired_rejected and superseded_duplicate replies remain explainable through MoreInfoResponseDisposition but may not implicitly reopen a closed or superseded cycle.
source_refs: phase-3-the-human-checkpoint.md#3D More-info loop, patient response threading, and re-safety

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: The case could look complete after a dial action or local note without proving the callback outcome.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Callback completion requires CallbackOutcomeEvidenceBundle plus CallbackResolutionGate(decision = complete).

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: The thread could close on transport acceptance, local send acknowledgement, or optimistic timeline copy.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Delivery, dispute, reply review, repair routing, and closure are all gated by evidence bundles and ThreadResolutionGate.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Provider acceptance or local success toast could imply booked truth before authoritative confirmation.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Only strong booking truth may enter booked. Otherwise remain confirmation_pending or supplier_reconciliation_pending with an ExternalConfirmationGate.
source_refs: phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4A Booking contract, case model, and state machine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Booking-domain code could write canonical Request.workflowState directly when booked or reconciled.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Booking emits branch-local truth and milestone evidence only; LifecycleCoordinator alone derives request-level milestone change.
source_refs: phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4A Booking contract, case model, and state machine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A local waitlist could claim the callback or hub debt is gone before the new branch is actually linked and patient-visible.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Fallback obligation remains explicit through transfer_pending and transferred until the new continuation path is durably linked.
source_refs: phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4G Smart Waitlist and local auto-fill
phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5G No-slot handling, urgent bounce-back, callback fallback, and reopen mechanics

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: The case could look fully booked while practice acknowledgement debt or fallback linkage is still unresolved.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Keep booked_pending_practice_ack explicit and do not close the hub branch until acknowledgement or policy exception is current for the latest generation.
source_refs: phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5F Native hub booking commit, practice continuity, and cross-org messaging
phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5H Patient communications, network reminders, manage flows, and practice visibility

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Hub code could overwrite canonical request workflow or closure meaning directly from hub-local truth.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Hub emits case-local truth and milestone evidence only; LifecycleCoordinator derives request-level milestone and closure semantics.
source_refs: phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A hub-native booking could jump straight to confirmed calmness without explicit confirmation and practice visibility stages.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Use confirmation_pending and confirmed_pending_practice_ack explicitly, then clear only on current-generation acknowledgement or policy exception.
source_refs: phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5F Native hub booking commit, practice continuity, and cross-org messaging

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Transport or provider acceptance could be mistaken for authoritative dispatch truth.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Only proof-satisfied dispatch on the current tuple may move the case into consultation_outcome_pending; weak modes stay behind ExternalConfirmationGate and reconciliation review when needed.
source_refs: phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Weak pharmacy outcome truth could directly advance canonical request workflow.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: outcome_reconciliation_pending stays case-local. LifecycleCoordinator derives request milestones only from settled outcome evidence and blocker evaluation.
source_refs: phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A stale or expired consent could silently remain good enough for dispatch.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Any renewed consent must produce a fresh consent record, checkpoint, and package fingerprint on the current provider/pathway scope.
source_refs: phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6A Pharmacy contract, case model, and state machine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Transport or provider acceptance alone could be treated as authoritative dispatch proof.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Dispatch truth remains explicit: acceptance may widen pending guidance but proof_satisfied needs the configured confirmation gate and tuple-bound proof.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Internal admin work could appear complete without a typed completion artifact or patient-visible expectation text.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: completed requires AdminResolutionSettlement(result = completed) and a matching AdminResolutionCompletionArtifact.

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Stale assistive artifacts could regain interactive posture just because the UI refreshed.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: When trust, publication, selected-anchor ownership, or continuity drift, freeze in place and require fresh current watch-tuple proof or rerun before trusted posture returns.
source_refs: phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8A Assistive capability contract, intended-use boundaries, and policy envelope

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Feature flags or local overrides could widen visible capability without satisfying the governed rollout ladder.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: AssistiveRolloutLadderPolicy is the only authority for widening posture from shadow to visible summary, insert, or governed commit.
source_refs: phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8A Assistive capability contract, intended-use boundaries, and policy envelope
phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#8I Pilot rollout, controlled slices, and formal exit gate

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Retention could delete replay-critical or legally preserved artifacts while the evidence graph still depends on them.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: DispositionEligibilityAssessment is the only delete gate and must converge freeze, legal-hold, dependency, and graph posture before deletion becomes legal.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#Records lifecycle, retention, legal hold, and deletion engine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Operators could treat hold release alone as permission to archive or delete.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: Hold release requires a fresh superseding DispositionEligibilityAssessment before any queued archive or delete job may proceed.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#Records lifecycle, retention, legal hold, and deletion engine

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Restore, failover, or chaos controls could rearm while recovery evidence or publication tuple is stale.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: bindingState = live is legal only when the linked ReleaseTrustFreezeVerdict is live, latest recovery evidence pack is current, and the binding tuple matches the current resilience tuple.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: A historical recovery pack could re-enable restore or failover controls after tuple drift.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: RecoveryEvidencePack is admissible only while packState = current and its resilienceTupleHash still matches live runtime truth.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

illegal_transition_rows.dangerous_interpretation: Narrative confidence or dashboard green could imply programme completion without exact continuity, runtime, and end-state proof.
illegal_transition_rows.canonical_correction: CrossPhaseConformanceScorecard becomes exact only when every required row is exact and the scorecard hash still matches current planning, verification, runtime publication, continuity evidence, and end-state proof.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9I Full-program exercises, BAU transfer, and formal exit gate

state_term_conflicts.dangerous_interpretation: The token `active` appears in multiple machines and can be misread as one shared status axis.
state_term_conflicts.canonical_correction: Always qualify duplicated state terms by owning machine and object; never mirror the label across machines without tuple and proof context.
